24	  const controller = new AbortController()

27	  // just us finishing the stream on our side. If `close` fires first, then we
28	  // know the client disconnected before we finished.
29	  response.once('close', () => {
30	    if (response.writableFinished) return

32	    controller.abort(new ResponseAborted())
33	  })

35	  return controller

export interface Geo {
  /** ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code, e.g. `"US"` */
  country?: string
  /** ISO 3166-2 region/subdivision code, e.g. `"CA"` */
  region?: string
  /** City name, e.g. `"San Francisco"` */
  city?: string
  /** Latitude as a decimal string, e.g. `"37.7749"` */
  latitude?: string
  /** Longitude as a decimal string, e.g. `"-122.4194"` */

  /** IANA timezone string, e.g. `"America/Los_Angeles"` */

/**
 * Extracts geolocation data from CDN request headers.
 *
 * Reads Vercel `x-vercel-ip-*` headers first; falls back to the Cloudflare
 * `cf-ipcountry` header for the country code when no Vercel country header is
 * present.
 *
 * @param source - A Web API `Request` or `Headers` object.
 * @returns A {@link Geo} object; fields absent from the headers are `undefined`.
 *
 * @example
 * ```ts
 * import { getGeo } from 'next/geo'
 *
 * export function middleware(req: NextRequest) {
 *   const { country, city } = getGeo(req)
 *   console.log(`Request from ${city}, ${country}`)
 * }
 * ```
 */
export function getGeo(source: Request | Headers): Geo {
  const headers = source instanceof Headers ? source : source.headers

  const country =
    headers.get('x-vercel-ip-country') ??
    headers.get('cf-ipcountry') ??
    undefined

  const region = headers.get('x-vercel-ip-country-region') ?? undefined
  const city = headers.get('x-vercel-ip-city') ?? undefined
  const latitude = headers.get('x-vercel-ip-latitude') ?? undefined
  const longitude = headers.get('x-vercel-ip-longitude') ?? undefined
  const timezone = headers.get('x-vercel-ip-timezone') ?? undefined

  return { country, region, city, latitude, longitude, timezone }
}

/**
 * Returns the localised display name for an ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code.
 *
 * Uses the platform's `Intl.DisplayNames` API and returns `undefined` when the
 * code is unrecognised or when the API is unavailable.
 *
 * @param code   - ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code, e.g. `"US"` (case-insensitive).
 * @param locale - BCP 47 locale tag for the output language (default: `"en"`).
 * @returns The localised country name, or `undefined` if it cannot be resolved.
 *
 * @example
 * ```ts
 * getCountryName('DE')        // "Germany"
 * getCountryName('DE', 'de')  // "Deutschland"
 * getCountryName('fr')        // "France"  (lowercase accepted)
 * ```
 */
export function getCountryName(
  code: string,
  locale: string = 'en'
): string | undefined {
  if (
    typeof Intl === 'undefined' ||
    typeof Intl.DisplayNames === 'undefined'
  ) {
    return undefined
  }
  try {
    const display = new Intl.DisplayNames([locale], { type: 'region' })
    return display.of(code.toUpperCase())
  } catch {
    return undefined
  }
}

/**
 * Returns `true` when `countryCode` belongs to the named `region`.
 *
 * The lookup is case-insensitive (`"fr"` and `"FR"` both match).
 *
 * @param countryCode - ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code.
 * @param region      - One of the supported {@link GeoRegion} identifiers.
 *
 * @example
 * ```ts
 * isInRegion('FR', 'EU')   // true
 * isInRegion('NO', 'EU')   // false
 * isInRegion('NO', 'EEA')  // true
 * isInRegion('jp', 'APAC') // true  (lowercase accepted)
 * ```
 */
export function isInRegion(countryCode: string, region: GeoRegion): boolean {
  const members = REGION_MEMBERS[region]
  if (!members) return false
  return members.has(countryCode.toUpperCase())
}

/**
 * A pair of geographic coordinates in decimal degrees.
 */
export interface LatLon {
  /** Latitude in decimal degrees (−90 to 90). */
  lat: number
  /** Longitude in decimal degrees (−180 to 180). */
  lon: number
}

/**
 * Calculates the great-circle distance between two points on Earth using the
 * Haversine formula.
 *
 * @param from - Starting coordinates `{ lat, lon }`.
 * @param to   - Ending coordinates `{ lat, lon }`.
 * @param unit - Distance unit: `"km"` (default) or `"mi"`.
 * @returns Distance rounded to two decimal places in the requested unit.
 *
 * @example
 * ```ts
 * const london = { lat: 51.5074, lon: -0.1278 }
 * const paris  = { lat: 48.8566, lon:  2.3522 }
 *
 * haversineDistance(london, paris)       // ~341.37  (km)
 * haversineDistance(london, paris, 'mi') // ~212.12  (miles)
 * haversineDistance(london, london)      // 0
 * ```
 */
export function haversineDistance(
  from: LatLon,
  to: LatLon,
  unit: 'km' | 'mi' = 'km'
): number {
  // Earth's mean radius
  const R = unit === 'mi' ? 3958.8 : 6371.0

  const toRad = (deg: number): number => (deg * Math.PI) / 180

  const dLat = toRad(to.lat - from.lat)
  const dLon = toRad(to.lon - from.lon)

  const sinHalfDLat = Math.sin(dLat / 2)
  const sinHalfDLon = Math.sin(dLon / 2)

    sinHalfDLat * sinHalfDLat +

/**
 * Geo information extracted from CDN request headers.
 */
export interface Geo {
  /** ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code, e.g. `"US"` */
  country?: string
  /** ISO 3166-2 region/subdivision code, e.g. `"CA"` */
  region?: string
  /** City name, e.g. `"San Francisco"` */
  city?: string
  /** Latitude as a decimal string, e.g. `"37.7749"` */
  latitude?: string
  /** Longitude as a decimal string, e.g. `"-122.4194"` */
  longitude?: string
  /** IANA timezone string, e.g. `"America/Los_Angeles"` */
  timezone?: string
}

/**
 * Named geographic or political regions accepted by {@link isInRegion}.
 *
 * - `"EU"` – 27 European Union member states
 * - `"EEA"` – European Economic Area (EU + IS, LI, NO)
 * - `"APAC"` – Asia-Pacific
 * - `"LATAM"` – Latin America
 * - `"MENA"` – Middle East & North Africa
 * - `"NA"` – North America (CA, MX, US)
 * - `"AFRICA"` – Africa
 * - `"CARIBBEAN"` – Caribbean
 */
export type GeoRegion =

/**
 * Extracts geolocation data from CDN request headers.
 *
 * Reads Vercel `x-vercel-ip-*` headers first; falls back to the Cloudflare
 * `cf-ipcountry` header for the country code when no Vercel country header is
 * present.
 *
 * @param source - A Web API `Request` or `Headers` object.
 * @returns A {@link Geo} object; fields absent from the headers are `undefined`.
 *
 * @example
 * ```ts
 * import { getGeo } from 'next/geo'
 *
 * export function middleware(req: NextRequest) {
 *   const { country, city } = getGeo(req)
 *   console.log(`Request from ${city}, ${country}`)
 * }
 * ```
 */
export function getGeo(source: Request | Headers): Geo {
  const headers = source instanceof Headers ? source : source.headers

  const country =
    headers.get('x-vercel-ip-country') ??
    headers.get('cf-ipcountry') ??
    undefined

  const region = headers.get('x-vercel-ip-country-region') ?? undefined
  const city = headers.get('x-vercel-ip-city') ?? undefined
  const latitude = headers.get('x-vercel-ip-latitude') ?? undefined
  const longitude = headers.get('x-vercel-ip-longitude') ?? undefined
  const timezone = headers.get('x-vercel-ip-timezone') ?? undefined

  return { country, region, city, latitude, longitude, timezone }
}

/**
 * Returns the localised display name for an ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code.
 *
 * Uses the platform's `Intl.DisplayNames` API and returns `undefined` when the
 * code is unrecognised or when the API is unavailable.
 *
 * @param code   - ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code, e.g. `"US"` (case-insensitive).
 * @param locale - BCP 47 locale tag for the output language (default: `"en"`).
 * @returns The localised country name, or `undefined` if it cannot be resolved.
 *
 * @example
 * ```ts
 * getCountryName('DE')        // "Germany"
 * getCountryName('DE', 'de')  // "Deutschland"
 * getCountryName('fr')        // "France"  (lowercase accepted)
 * ```
 */
export function getCountryName(
  code: string,
  locale: string = 'en'
): string | undefined {
  if (
    typeof Intl === 'undefined' ||
    typeof Intl.DisplayNames === 'undefined'
  ) {
    return undefined
  }
  try {
    const display = new Intl.DisplayNames([locale], { type: 'region' })
    return display.of(code.toUpperCase())
  } catch {
    return undefined
  }
}

/**
 * Returns `true` when `countryCode` belongs to the named `region`.
 *
 * The lookup is case-insensitive (`"fr"` and `"FR"` both match).
 *
 * @param countryCode - ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code.
 * @param region      - One of the supported {@link GeoRegion} identifiers.
 *
 * @example
 * ```ts
 * isInRegion('FR', 'EU')   // true
 * isInRegion('NO', 'EU')   // false
 * isInRegion('NO', 'EEA')  // true
 * isInRegion('jp', 'APAC') // true  (lowercase accepted)
 * ```
 */
export function isInRegion(countryCode: string, region: GeoRegion): boolean {
  const members = REGION_MEMBERS[region]
  if (!members) return false
  return members.has(countryCode.toUpperCase())
}

/**
 * A pair of geographic coordinates in decimal degrees.
 */
export interface LatLon {
  /** Latitude in decimal degrees (−90 to 90). */
  lat: number
  /** Longitude in decimal degrees (−180 to 180). */
  lon: number
}

/**
 * Calculates the great-circle distance between two points on Earth using the
 * Haversine formula.
 *
 * @param from - Starting coordinates `{ lat, lon }`.
 * @param to   - Ending coordinates `{ lat, lon }`.
 * @param unit - Distance unit: `"km"` (default) or `"mi"`.
 * @returns Distance rounded to two decimal places in the requested unit.
 *
 * @example
 * ```ts
 * const london = { lat: 51.5074, lon: -0.1278 }
 * const paris  = { lat: 48.8566, lon:  2.3522 }
 *
 * haversineDistance(london, paris)       // ~341.37  (km)
 * haversineDistance(london, paris, 'mi') // ~212.12  (miles)
 * haversineDistance(london, london)      // 0
 * ```
 */
export function haversineDistance(
  from: LatLon,
  to: LatLon,
  unit: 'km' | 'mi' = 'km'
): number {
  // Earth's mean radius
  const R = unit === 'mi' ? 3958.8 : 6371.0

  const toRad = (deg: number): number => (deg * Math.PI) / 180

  const dLat = toRad(to.lat - from.lat)
  const dLon = toRad(to.lon - from.lon)

  const sinHalfDLat = Math.sin(dLat / 2)
  const sinHalfDLon = Math.sin(dLon / 2)

  const a =
    sinHalfDLat * sinHalfDLat +
    Math.cos(toRad(from.lat)) *
      Math.cos(toRad(to.lat)) *
      sinHalfDLon *
      sinHalfDLon

  const c = 2 * Math.atan2(Math.sqrt(a), Math.sqrt(1 - a))
  const distance = R * c

  return Math.round(distance * 100) / 100
}

Everything is in order. Here's a summary of every file created or modified:

---

### Files created

**`packages/next/src/server/request/geo.ts`** — the implementation  
Four named exports, all self-contained (no async-storage / render-cycle dependencies needed for a pure header-parsing utility):

| Export | Description |
|---|---|
| `Geo` | Interface: `country`, `region`, `city`, `latitude`, `longitude`, `timezone` (all optional strings) |
| `GeoRegion` | Union type: `'EU' \| 'EEA' \| 'APAC' \| 'LATAM' \| 'MENA' \| 'NA' \| 'AFRICA' \| 'CARIBBEAN'` |
| `LatLon` | Interface: `{ lat: number; lon: number }` |
| `getGeo(source)` | Reads Vercel `x-vercel-ip-*` headers; falls back to Cloudflare `cf-ipcountry` for country. Accepts a `Request` or bare `Headers` object. |
| `getCountryName(code, locale?)` | `Intl.DisplayNames`-based lookup → localised country name or `undefined` |
| `isInRegion(code, region)` | Set-based membership check; case-insensitive |
| `haversineDistance(from, to, unit?)` | Haversine formula → distance in km (default) or mi, rounded to 2 d.p. |

**`packages/next/src/api/geo.ts`** — thin re-export (`export * from '../server/request/geo'`)

**`packages/next/geo.js`** — CommonJS entry point (mirrors the `headers.js` pattern with individual `module.exports.*` assignments pointing at `./dist/server/request/geo`)

**`packages/next/geo.d.ts`** — TypeScript declarations (`export * from './dist/server/request/geo'`)

**`test/unit/next-geo.test.ts`** — comprehensive unit-test suite covering all four helpers: Vercel/Cloudflare header extraction, fallback priority, `Request` vs `Headers` overloads, country-name lookup, every named region (membership + rejection + case-insensitivity), and Haversine (identity, symmetry, km↔mi conversion, long-haul sanity range, rounding)

### File modified

**`packages/next/package.json`** — `"geo.js"` and `"geo.d.ts"` added to the `"files"` array (all existing entries unchanged)
